notes: Fix the self-dismissing SW UPDATE pill so pending updates can actually be activated from it. Root cause: sw_pillRefresh(forceState) sets the #sw-status-pill to state 'update' when a new SW is waiting (via the update-available handler), but the stateless callers -- the 5s visibility-gated poll, the visibilitychange handler, the initial 200ms call, and sw_pillTap's else branch -- all call sw_pillRefresh() with no argument, which falls through to the 'ready' branch (a controller exists) and downgrades the pill from SW UPDATE back to SW READY within <=5s. So the violet UPDATE pill flashed and vanished before it could be tapped, blocking SKIP_WAITING activation. FIX: one guard at the top of sw_pillRefresh -- if forceState is not 'update' AND the pill is already data-sw-state='update', return early (preserve the pending-update affordance). Only an explicit forceState('update') re-affirms it; applying the update reloads the page, which starts fresh with no 'update' state, so the pill correctly returns to READY afterward -- no stuck-update risk. JS-only, one function; no legacy path touched. ?legacy=1 byte-identical. This is the SIDE FIX from the legacy-retirement handoff, shipped independently ahead of the retirement phases (Phase 0 census is the next gate and deletes nothing until John signs off). DEVICE-VERIFY (HARD-STOP): trigger an update (deploy a newer stamp, reopen the installed PWA), confirm the violet SW UPDATE pill PERSISTS (no longer vanishes), tap it, confirm it reloads onto the new version. ROLLBACK: revert the commit.
